title: Thawing Frozen Adherent Cell Lines

steps: Day 1 Prepare media ahead of time and warm to room temperature Spray down each bottle with 70% Ethanol before placing it into the TC hood Pre-cool the centrifuge to 4ºC Prepare and label a 6-well plate, and aliquot 9 mL of TCM into a 15 mL conical for each tube of cells to be thawed Find the location of the cells you will be thawing in the liquid nitrogen inventory google doc Transfer the cells between the liquid nitrogen and the TC room on ice Record the information on the cryotube – date, number of cells, etc Begin adding media from the 15mL conical to the still frozen cells, then transfer media back and forth between the two tubes until the cells have completely thawed and everything has been transferred to the 15 mL conical Spin down the cells at 1200rpm for 10’ at 4ºC Pour off the supernatant and break up the cell pellets by tapping the tubes Add 1 mL of TCM to the cells, pipette up and down to break up any clumps, add 9 mL TCM, and transfer 1-2 mL of resuspended cells to a 6-well plate (cells should reach 80% confluency in two days) Add 10 µl of Mycoplasma removal agent per 1 mL of media Confirm the presence of cells in the well by microscope Incubate at 37ºC

Day 3 * If cells tested negative for mycoplasma before freezing, only 1 round of MRA treatment is necessary. If mycoplasma presence is questionable or unknown 2 rounds of MRA treatment should be done – see mycoplasma protocol for more information. Warm media to room temperature Remove media and wash cells once with PBS Add 1 mL of 0.05% Trypsin-EDTA to wells and incubate plate at 37ºC for 1-3 minutes until cells are completely released from the plate Add 4 mL of media to wells to halt trypsinization and transfer to a T25 flask Incubate at 37ºC

Day 4 Warm media to room temperature Check for cell adherence If there are a lot of dead cells, change the media on the cells If cells are nearing 50% confluence, trypsinize, transfer in 10 mL to a T75 flask – Label as passage #0
creator: Carolina Lopez
